skill.name: data-engineering-data-pipeline
skill.description: Guidance for designing reliable batch and streaming data pipelines with orchestration, quality checks, monitoring, and cost controls.
skill.summary: Design scalable data pipelines with practical architecture, implementation, quality, and operations guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported external command, weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found these are false positives caused by markdown fences and ordinary data engineering terminology, with no executable command, malware intent, prompt injection, or exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fence Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged line opens a Python markdown example. The surrounding example shows illustrative data pipeline code and does not run shell commands or interpolate user input into commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is a fenced code block marker, not Ruby or shell execution. No command execution API or subprocess call appears in the surrounding example.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Matches Are Data Terms
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain normal data pipeline language such as design, data flow, ACID transactions, and failure modes. No cryptographic algorithm or secret handling is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptographic use. The matches are substrings inside benign documentation terms.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Patterns Are Operations Guidance
low_findings.description: The flagged lines discuss schema validation, partition sizing, and operational alerts. They do not instruct the agent to enumerate hosts, scan networks, or collect system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is data pipeline reliability and monitoring. No evidence found of system reconnaissance, port scanning, network probing, or data exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Data teams need pipeline designs that handle growth, failures, and cost pressure. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create practical batch and streaming architectures.
actual_capabilities: Designs ETL, ELT, Lambda, Kappa, and Lakehouse pipeline patterns.
Plans batch and streaming ingestion with retries, watermarks, and replay support.
Recommends Airflow, Prefect, dbt, Spark, Delta Lake, and Iceberg patterns.
Defines data quality checks with Great Expectations and dbt tests.
Outlines monitoring, alerting, cost optimization, and operations deliverables.
limitations: Does not include runnable source files beyond an illustrative markdown example.
Requires project-specific source systems, volumes, latency targets, and compliance needs.
Generated code still needs security, performance, and production review.
Does not provision cloud infrastructure or connect to live data systems.

use_cases.title: Modernize Batch Reporting
use_cases.description: Plan an incremental ELT pipeline with orchestration, quality checks, storage layout, and monitoring.

use_cases.title: Build Streaming Ingestion
use_cases.description: Design Kafka-based ingestion with exactly-once handling, replay, dead letter queues, and freshness alerts.

use_cases.title: Review Pipeline Reliability
use_cases.description: Assess failure modes, recovery procedures, operational dashboards, and cost controls for an existing pipeline.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Pipeline Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose ETL, ELT, Lambda, Kappa, or Lakehouse for these sources, volumes, latency needs, and targets.

prompt_templates.title: Design Batch Ingestion
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an incremental batch ingestion pipeline with watermarks, validation, retries, dead letter handling, and storage layout.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Operations Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create monitoring, alerting, rollback, troubleshooting, and cost optimization guidance for this data pipeline architecture.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Complex Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this proposed batch and streaming pipeline for reliability, scalability, data quality, cost, and operational risk.

output_examples.input: Design an ELT pipeline for daily orders from Postgres into a Lakehouse warehouse.
output_examples.output: Architecture summary, ingestion steps, dbt model layers, quality checks, partition strategy, monitoring metrics, and recovery procedures.

output_examples.input: Review a Kafka pipeline for clickstream events with a 10 minute freshness target.
output_examples.output: Streaming design review, offset handling guidance, replay strategy, failure modes, alert thresholds, dashboard metrics, and scaling notes.

output_examples.input: Prepare production readiness guidance for a Spark and Delta Lake pipeline.
output_examples.output: Operational checklist covering deployment, retries, compaction, schema evolution, cost controls, access review, and incident response.

best_practices: Provide source systems, data volume, latency targets, and destination requirements before requesting an architecture.
Ask for explicit failure modes, recovery steps, and alert thresholds for production pipelines.
Validate generated implementation details against your cloud platform, access model, and compliance requirements.
anti_patterns: Do not request production code without providing real schemas, credentials strategy, and operational constraints.
Do not skip data quality checks for high value reporting or customer-facing data products.
Do not use the example connection strings or storage paths as production configuration.

faq.question: Can this skill generate complete pipeline code?
faq.answer: It can help draft implementation patterns, but the skill itself only provides guidance and an illustrative example.

faq.question: Does it support both batch and streaming data?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers batch ingestion, streaming ingestion, orchestration, storage, quality, monitoring, and operations.

faq.question: Which tools does it mention?
faq.answer: It references Airflow, Prefect, dbt, Spark, Great Expectations, Delta Lake, Iceberg, CloudWatch, Prometheus, and Grafana.

faq.question: Can it choose a cloud provider for me?
faq.answer: It can compare architecture options, but provider selection needs your budget, compliance, region, and team constraints.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Manual review found no confirmed malicious behavior, command execution, credential theft, or prompt injection.

faq.question: What should I provide in a prompt?
faq.answer: Provide source systems, schemas, volume, latency target, destination, quality requirements, and operational constraints.
